name: Afroasiatic (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a language). Afroasiatic (Afro-Asiatic) is the phylum comprising Berber, Semitic, Egyptian, Cushitic, Omotic and Chadic. It exists here only so that the ancestry/phylogenetic-distance metrics have a top-level node to traverse to for its member families (Berber, Semitic/Arabic, etc.); no phonology is claimed or reconstructed for it, and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder. Glottolog top node afro1255.

sources.author: Frajzyngier, Z. & Shay, E. (eds.)
sources.title: The Afroasiatic Languages
sources.notes: Reference for the Afroasiatic phylum and its internal branching (structural ancestry node only).
